skill.name: cost-optimization
skill.description: Guides cloud cost reduction through rightsizing, tagging, reserved capacity, storage lifecycle rules, budget alerts, and governance practices across AWS, Azure, and GCP.
skill.summary: Plan practical cloud cost reductions across AWS, Azure, and GCP.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static findings are false positives caused by Markdown backticks, cloud product names, and Terraform example text in SKILL.md. No prompt injection, credential access, network exfiltration, executable scripts, or malicious intent was found in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backtick Examples
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution, but these locations are Markdown inline references or fenced examples. They do not execute commands and show cost optimization guidance or Terraform snippets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged syntax is Markdown formatting, not a Ruby file or executable shell context. The surrounding text contains static examples and references only.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Cloud Service Keywords
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged C2 keywords, but the cited lines refer to AWS EC2, Fargate, Lambda, and an AWS CloudWatch namespace. These are normal cloud service names in a cost optimization guide.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The terms appear in ordinary AWS pricing and monitoring examples. No command and control endpoint, network beacon, or covert communication behavior is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keyword
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged a weak cryptographic algorithm at the frontmatter description. The line describes cloud cost optimization and contains no cryptographic operation or algorithm selection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is descriptive metadata, not code. I found no evidence of MD5, SHA-1, insecure encryption, or related cryptographic implementation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Terraform Resource Text
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged system reconnaissance, but the cited lines are Terraform attributes or an Azure Hybrid Benefit heading. They do not inspect the local system or collect host data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is infrastructure documentation, not executable reconnaissance logic. No local file, process, user, or network inventory collection is present.

content.user_title: Reduce Cloud Spend With Cost Governance
content.value_statement: Cloud teams often overspend because resource use, tags, and pricing commitments are hard to review consistently. This skill provides structured guidance for rightsizing, storage policies, discounts, monitoring, and governance across major clouds.
actual_capabilities: Explains rightsizing, autoscaling, and idle resource cleanup for cloud workloads.
Compares AWS, Azure, and GCP discount models for steady and interruptible workloads.
Provides Terraform examples for S3 lifecycle rules, tags, budgets, and autoscaling.
Outlines tagging standards for cost allocation and ownership tracking.
Lists monitoring tools and budget alert practices for cloud cost governance.
Summarizes architecture patterns that reduce idle capacity and storage cost.
limitations: It does not connect to cloud accounts or retrieve live billing data.
Savings percentages are general examples and may change by region or service.
Terraform snippets need review before use in production environments.
Referenced supporting files are not included in the scanned package.

use_cases.title: Plan a FinOps Review
use_cases.description: Create a structured checklist for tags, budgets, reserved capacity, idle resources, and storage lifecycle policies.

use_cases.title: Right-Size Cloud Resources
use_cases.description: Identify practical actions for reducing over-provisioned compute, database, and storage resources without harming reliability.

use_cases.title: Set Cost Governance Standards
use_cases.description: Define common controls for ownership tags, budget alerts, anomaly detection, and weekly cost review routines.

prompt_templates.title: Beginner Cost Checklist
prompt_templates.scenario: You need a first pass cost review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a cloud cost optimization checklist for my AWS, Azure, or GCP environment. Include rightsizing, tags, alerts, storage, and discount options.

prompt_templates.title: Rightsizing Review
prompt_templates.scenario: You have utilization data and need action items.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these resource utilization notes and recommend rightsizing actions. Separate low-risk changes from changes that need load testing or approval.

prompt_templates.title: Governance Rollout
prompt_templates.scenario: You need standards for cost ownership.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a cost governance plan for a platform team. Include required tags, budget alerts, anomaly detection, review cadence, and owner responsibilities.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Cloud Savings Strategy
prompt_templates.scenario: You need a detailed optimization roadmap.
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a 90-day multi-cloud cost optimization roadmap. Compare reserved capacity, savings plans, committed use discounts, spot workloads, storage tiers, and architecture changes.

output_examples.input: Review our monthly cloud spend process and suggest improvements.
output: Start with cost allocation tags for environment, project, owner, and cost center.
Add monthly budgets with alerts at 80 percent and 100 percent.
Review idle disks, unused addresses, oversized databases, and long-running development resources.
Evaluate reserved capacity only after confirming steady usage patterns.

output_examples.input: Create a storage optimization plan for object storage.
output: Classify data as hot, warm, cold, or archive.
Move warm data to infrequent access after 30 days.
Move cold data to archive storage after 90 days.
Add expiration rules only after retention and compliance review.

output_examples.input: Help me compare cloud discount options.
output_examples.output: The response would compare commitment length, flexibility, workload fit, interruption risk, and approval needs across reserved instances, savings plans, committed use discounts, and spot or preemptible resources.

best_practices: Validate recommendations against current billing data before committing to long-term discounts.
Apply ownership and cost center tags before building dashboards or chargeback reports.
Test autoscaling and lifecycle policies in lower environments before production rollout.
anti_patterns: Buying reserved capacity before confirming stable baseline usage.
Deleting resources without checking backups, retention rules, or service dependencies.
Using spot or preemptible instances for stateful workloads without recovery design.

faq.question: Does this skill access my cloud billing account?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples, but it does not connect to cloud providers or retrieve billing data.

faq.question: Which cloud providers does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers AWS, Azure, and GCP at a guidance level, with some AWS-focused Terraform examples.

faq.question: Can it calculate exact savings?
faq.answer: No. It can structure the analysis, but exact savings require current pricing, usage, region, and contract details.

faq.question: Is it useful for FinOps programs?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports tagging, budget alerts, anomaly detection, review routines, and governance planning.

faq.question: Are the Terraform examples production ready?
faq.answer: No. They are examples that need security, compliance, naming, and environment review before use.

faq.question: Does it include detailed supporting resources?
faq.answer: No. The scanned package only includes SKILL.md, although the text references additional files.
